facts.text: Hortonville sits within the Stone Arch Bridge Historic Park, a landmark that celebrates the town’s rich river heritage.
The town’s River Management Plan protects the Delaware River, ensuring clean water and sustainable fishing spots for residents and visitors alike.
Spanning 35 scenic square miles, Hortonville is one of the charming hamlets that make up the town of Delaware in Sullivan County.
Officially founded in 1869, the area’s first settlers arrived as early as the 1600s, laying the groundwork for a long‑standing community.
The landscape ranges from flat, valley‑side views along the Delaware River to rolling hills that offer panoramic vistas of the surrounding countryside.
Hortonville’s town hall is located at 104 Main Street, a central address that serves as the hub for local governance and community events.
